summary: nolimit-x ships an obfuscated bulk email/SMS spam and phishing toolkit
details: The package self-describes as an 'Advanced email sender' with keywords including 'red-team' and ships 45 heavily obfuscated source files under.ad/ (javascript-obfuscator string-array+RC4 with control-flow flattening and self-defending wrappers). The runtime implements bulk SMTP/SMS sending with DKIM key generation, TLS-fingerprint spoofing (TLSFingerprintSpoofer in.ad/xj.js), envelope-from spoofing, raw SMTP / SMTP smuggling, stealth-mode delivery, and open-redirect scanning..ad/web-command.js builds a Chrome extension at ~/nolimit-web-ext that connects to ws://localhost:9227 and injects panel code into Gmail, Outlook, and Yahoo webmail tabs..ad/x12.js implements `extractContacts`, which uses hardcoded Microsoft and Google OAuth client_ids to drive device-code flows and harvest contacts and recipient lists from compromised mailboxes via Microsoft Graph (/me/contacts, /me/people, /me/messages, /me/mailFolders/sentitems/messages), the Google People API, and IMAP (Sent/INBOX.Sent/Sent Items)..ad/x0.js and.ad/x4.js POST {key, hwid, hostname, platform} to an author-controlled license server whose URL is hidden in the obfuscated string array. Installing this package brings a fully offensive phishing/spam framework with mailbox-harvesting and webmail-injection capabilities into the consumer's build environment; the deliberate obfuscation is concealment of that capability.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
